title: West African Chicken Peanut Stew

overview.description: A rich and comforting chicken stew made with tomatoes, peanut butter, sweet potatoes and warming spices. Creamy without using dairy, it's one of West Africa's best-loved family meals.

ingredients: 2 medium sweet potatoes, peeled and diced
method: Heat the oil in a large casserole dish over a medium-high heat. Brown the chicken on both sides, then remove and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and cook the onion for about 8 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander and chilli flakes. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for another minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes, stock and sweet potatoes, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Return the chicken to the pan, cover and cook for 30 minutes.
Whisk the peanut butter with a ladle of the hot cooking liquid until smooth, then stir it back into the stew.
Add the chickpeas and simmer uncovered for a further 15 minutes until the sauce is rich and the sweet potatoes are tender.
Season to taste and scatter with fresh coriander before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Groundnuts (peanuts) were introduced to West Africa centuries ago and are now a staple ingredient in many regional stews and soups.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How peanut butter can create a silky, deeply savoury sauce, why it should be mixed with hot stock before adding, and how sweet potato balances the richness naturally.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A fantastic introduction to another corner of African cooking. It's hearty, nutritious, freezes exceptionally well and uses ingredients that are easy to find in UK supermarkets.
chapman-tips: Use 100% peanut butter if possible for the cleanest flavour.
Whisking the peanut butter with hot stock prevents lumps.
Add a squeeze of lime before serving for extra freshness if you have one.
notes.batch-cooking: Ideal for batch cooking. Freeze in family portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: The sauce becomes even richer overnight and reheats beautifully.
